behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS is present: this is cross-module state handoff (git-derived branch facts + age threshold + PR presence). REAL-INFRA is not explicitly required by the behavior text (no "live"/real-network requirement), so System was considered but rejected because the behavior can be validated with in-process module wiring and fixture branch/PR data. LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP are not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real staleness detector with real internal collaborators (branch-state computation + threshold config + PR-state provider adapter stubbed at boundary) and validate outcomes for representative branch/age/PR combinations end-to-end within the app module graph.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is in module interaction (bootstrap/session-start orchestration + stale-state signal + renderer/message formatting), not a single pure function. System was considered, but rejected because the behavior text does not require real OS/process/network boundaries; startup can be invoked in-process with dependency wiring. No LLM dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke real session-start setup path in a test harness with a precomputed stale current-branch state and assert emitted warning content includes actionable branch context (branch name, age/signal, next action).

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly a local deterministic mapping function with no I/O, no multi-module handoff requirement, and no external infra/model dependency. Higher levels add no new reality signal for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Pure function tests over exhaustive/representative branch-state inputs asserting exact label outputs and deterministic repeatability.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly requires "against live repo and PR state". REAL-INFRA applies (real git/repo + real PR source/network). ROUND-TRIP externally visible effect is required: rendered command output must reflect real state. Integration with mocks could hide contract/state-sync failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` in a real repository context connected to real PR state, covering both scenarios (clean vs commits-without-PR), and assert rendered output text/section marks STALE only in the latter.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is correct orchestration/logic across branch-state and PR-status interpretation. System was considered but rejected because behavior text does not require live external API verification; representative open/merged states can be validated through integrated module wiring. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise the staleness pipeline with branch cases linked to PR states `open` and `merged` and assert classifier/output never emits `stale-no-pr` for those cases.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: rebases/squash merges are git history operations whose failure modes often appear only with real git subprocess/repo behavior. Mocks of commit graphs can hide edge cases in ancestry/hash rewriting. This is not agentic.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo test fixture, perform actual rebase and squash-merge workflows, then run staleness detection and verify post-operation branch state is not classified as unPRed stale when work is effectively incorporated.
